issues: sparse orange accent dots may form a subtle repeating pattern at large tile counts
minor density variation: bottom-right quadrant appears slightly busier than top-left
reasoning: GEOMETRY CHECK: (1) Column 0 vs column 31 — both show the same mid-green base with scattered 1-2px lighter green specks; no obvious seam break, edge continuity looks solid. (2) Row 0 vs row 31 — top and bottom rows are visually consistent mid-green noise; no brightness discontinuity detected. (3) 3x3 mental tile: the orange/yellow accent pixels (~8-10 per tile) are spread unevenly — a slight cluster near upper-center and lower-right would produce a faint diagonal polka rhythm at 3x3+ scale, not egregious but noticeable on close inspection. DENSITY: overall balanced — green noise fills the field uniformly, no large empty swaths or dense hot-spots. The lighter green sub-pixel speckle gives organic variation. BACKGROUND QUALITY: strong ground-tile candidate; the muted palette recedes behind sprites. The orange dots are the only mild concern — they are small but high-contrast, and their non-uniform scatter means they could form a subtle repeating dot grid artifact when the tile is used at scale. Score 7: passes basic seamlessness and density criteria but orange dot clustering prevents a clean pass.
